wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AutoPoV project overview section focused on the autonomous vulnerability research platform. Explain its purpose as a multi-agent system built on LangGraph that orchestrates every stage of security workflow including code ingestion, static analysis, LLM-powered investigation, exploit generation, and execution validation. Document the agentic architecture with stateful agent graph, autonomous routing decisions, and external tool integration. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining what AutoPoV does and technical details for experienced developers covering the LangGraph-based orchestration. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including agent nodes, policy agents, and learning stores. Provide practical examples demonstrating the complete vulnerability research workflow from code input to validated proof-of-vulnerability generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Graph Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's LangGraph-based agent orchestration system focused on stateful workflow management and conditional routing. Explain the AgentGraph class architecture, ScanState and VulnerabilityState data models, and the complete workflow from code ingestion through exploit validation. Document the state machine design with nodes for ingest_code, run_codeql, investigate, generate_pov, validate_pov, refine_pov, run_in_docker, and logging nodes. Detail the conditional edge logic including _should_generate_pov, _should_run_pov, _after_runtime_proof, and _has_more_findings decision points. Explain parallel processing capabilities with ThreadPoolExecutor, batch investigation strategies, and thread-safe operations. Include practical examples of workflow execution, state transitions, and error handling mechanisms. Document the integration with specialized agents through factory functions and dynamic agent instantiation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scan Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's scan initiation endpoints focused on comprehensive coverage of all scanning methods. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for scan-git (/api/scan/git), scan-zip (/api/scan/zip), and scan-paste (/api/scan/paste) endpoints. Include detailed parameter specifications for Git URLs, ZIP file uploads, and raw code pasting including optional parameters like branch specification, language detection, and model selection. Document request/response formats with Pydantic models, error handling strategies, and rate limiting behavior. Provide practical curl examples for each endpoint showing successful requests and common error scenarios. Include background task execution patterns, progress tracking mechanisms, and scan lifecycle management. Address file upload handling, repository accessibility checks, and model validation requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Environment Variables
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's core environment variables and configuration system. Document all essential environment variables including application settings (APP_NAME, DEBUG), API configuration (API_HOST, API_PORT), security credentials (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, WEBHOOK_SECRET), and file paths (DATA_DIR, RESULTS_DIR). Explain the Pydantic Settings pattern used for configuration management, environment variable precedence, and validation rules. Include practical examples of .env file configuration, variable naming conventions, and common configuration patterns. Address configuration loading order, default value handling, and environment-specific overrides. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common configuration issues and validation errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Graph Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's agent graph orchestration system focused on the LangGraph-based workflow management and stateful agent coordination. Explain the complete state machine architecture using ScanState and VulnerabilityState dictionaries, detailing the 10-node workflow including ingest_code, run_codeql, investigate, generate_pov, validate_pov, refine_pov, run_in_docker, and logging nodes. Document the conditional routing logic based on confidence thresholds, validation results, and runtime outcomes. Include implementation details for parallel processing capabilities, thread-safe batch investigation, and the self-healing refinement loop. Provide examples of state transitions, error handling mechanisms, and cancellation support. Address the intelligent model selection integration, token tracking across models, and cost optimization strategies. Document the workflow compilation process, node execution patterns, and the role of the Policy Agent in decision-making.

wiki_catalogs.name: Vector Database Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive vector database integration documentation for AutoPoV focused on ChromaDB configuration, embedding generation, and semantic search functionality. Document the codebase ingestion pipeline that processes source code files, generates embeddings using sentence-transformers models, and stores vectors in ChromaDB for semantic similarity search. Explain the embedding generation process, vector dimensionality, and similarity scoring mechanisms. Detail the integration between code ingestion agents and vector store operations, including chunking strategies, metadata preservation, and retrieval augmented generation (RAG) workflows. Include configuration options for embedding models, vector database parameters, and performance tuning. Document query optimization techniques, similarity thresholds, and relevance ranking. Address vector store maintenance, indexing strategies, and cleanup procedures. Provide troubleshooting guidance for embedding quality issues, memory management, and performance bottlenecks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's React application architecture focused on the overall system structure and component hierarchy. Describe the main App component with React Router configuration, route definitions, and nested routing pattern. Document the AppShell component as the primary layout container and its role in maintaining consistent navigation and styling across pages. Explain the routing strategy using React Router v6, including protected routes, parameterized routes for scan IDs, and nested layout structure. Detail the main.jsx entry point configuration, React application initialization, and integration with the build system. Include component hierarchy diagrams showing the relationship between App, AppShell, and page components. Address state management patterns, context providers if used, and the overall architectural approach for this full-stack React application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Container Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive container architecture documentation for AutoPoV focused on the multi-service Docker Compose setup. Document the three-container architecture including backend API service, frontend dashboard, and Ollama LLM service. Explain container interdependencies, network configuration, and service discovery mechanisms. Detail the volume mount strategy for persistent data storage (ChromaDB, Ollama models, results). Document environment variable configuration, health check implementations, and restart policies. Include container networking topology, port mappings, and service communication patterns. Address resource allocation strategies and container isolation principles. Provide guidance for understanding the container lifecycle and inter-service dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive production deployment documentation for AutoPoV focused on containerized deployment strategies and infrastructure setup. Document Docker Compose configuration including service definitions, port mappings, volume mounts, and environment variables. Explain backend and frontend container builds, health checks, and restart policies. Detail environment configuration including API settings, Docker socket mounting for sandbox execution, and model provider integration. Include infrastructure requirements covering CPU, memory, and storage specifications for different deployment scales. Document security considerations including network isolation, volume permissions, and API key management. Provide step-by-step deployment instructions for development, staging, and production environments. Address SSL/TLS configuration, reverse proxy setup, and load balancer integration. Include monitoring setup with health checks and alerting configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Model Routing & Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's intelligent model routing and optimization system. Document the Policy Agent's decision-making process for automatic model selection based on task complexity, cost analysis, and performance metrics. Explain the routing modes including fixed mode vs adaptive learning, model availability detection, and fallback mechanisms. Detail cost control strategies including token tracking, budget limits, and real-time cost monitoring. Document performance optimization techniques such as parallel processing configuration, worker scaling, and resource allocation. Include practical examples of model configuration, routing rule tuning, and cost optimization scenarios. Address advanced topics like model preference algorithms, learning-based routing improvements, and integration with external cost monitoring systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Interface Contract
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AutoPoV agent interface contract and base class requirements. Detail the mandatory methods that all agents must implement including state management, execution lifecycle, and data processing interfaces. Document the agent registration system and how agents integrate with the LangGraph orchestration framework. Explain the state object structure, parameter validation, and return value conventions. Include code examples showing proper interface implementation patterns. Document the agent factory pattern and how new agents are instantiated and managed by the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Policy Decision Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AutoPoV's policy decision algorithms that govern automatic model selection. Document the Policy Agent's decision-making framework including task complexity assessment, model capability matching, and routing mode selection between fixed and adaptive learning. Explain the model resolution logic in resolve_model_mode() method, including online/offline model validation and fallback mechanisms. Detail the routing decision criteria based on scan complexity, finding confidence thresholds, and resource requirements. Include practical examples of policy configuration, decision flowcharts, and integration with the agent orchestration system. Address edge cases like model unavailability, performance degradation detection, and dynamic routing adjustments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Code Ingestion Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Code Ingestion Agent responsible for codebase analysis, chunking, and ChromaDB vector store integration. Document the CodeIngester class architecture, file processing pipeline, recursive character text splitting with language-aware separators, embedding generation using multiple backends (OpenAI, HuggingFace, SentenceTransformers, Hash-based), and ChromaDB persistent storage management. Include detailed coverage of supported programming languages, file filtering mechanisms, binary file detection, metadata handling, batch processing optimization, and error handling strategies. Provide implementation examples showing directory ingestion workflows, context retrieval patterns, and collection management operations. Address configuration options for chunk size, overlap, embedding models, and performance tuning for large codebases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for AutoPoV focused on installation, setup, and initial configuration. Document prerequisites including Python 3.11+, Node.js 20+, Docker Desktop, and API keys. Provide step-by-step installation instructions covering backend setup with virtual environment activation and requirements installation, frontend setup with npm dependencies, and environment configuration through .env file editing. Include initial system startup procedures using run.sh script for different modes (both, backend, frontend). Document API key provisioning through CLI and REST API methods. Provide first scan tutorial showing web dashboard usage and CLI commands. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for smooth onboarding.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for AutoPoV's component relationships and system boundaries focused on the layered architecture and component interactions. Describe the FastAPI backend as the central controller, specialized agent components for vulnerability detection, and external tool integrations. Document the component hierarchy from API endpoints through agent orchestration to specialized agents and external systems. Explain the dependency injection patterns, service layer architecture, and how components communicate through well-defined interfaces. Detail the integration points with Docker containers, CodeQL static analysis, ChromaDB vector store, and LLM providers. Include system boundary definitions, data flow patterns between components, and the plugin architecture for extensible agent development. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, logging, error handling, and configuration management across all components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Status & Monitoring Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's status monitoring and real-time streaming endpoints focused on comprehensive coverage of scan progress tracking and result retrieval. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and response schemas for scan-status (/api/scan/{scan_id}), log-streaming (/api/scan/{scan_id}/stream), and active-scans (/api/scans/active) endpoints. Include detailed response formats with progress percentages, status indicators, log arrays, and finding collections. Document Server-Sent Events (SSE) implementation for real-time log streaming including event types, message formats, and connection handling. Provide practical examples showing curl commands for status polling and SSE connections. Include error handling for missing scans, completed scans, and connection timeouts. Address streaming best practices, connection management, and client-side event processing patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Routing & Model Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AutoPoV's intelligent model routing and configuration system. Document the three routing modes (auto, fixed, learning) with detailed behavioral descriptions, use case recommendations, and configuration examples. Explain model mode selection between online (OpenRouter) and offline (Ollama) modes, including supported models and their characteristics. Document the model resolution logic, automatic mode detection, and manual overrides. Include configuration examples for different deployment scenarios, model performance characteristics, and cost optimization strategies. Address model availability detection, fallback mechanisms, and troubleshooting model connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Agents
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AutoPoV's specialized agent system covering all individual agent capabilities and their specific roles in the vulnerability detection pipeline. Document the Ingestion Agent for codebase analysis and ChromaDB vector store integration, Scout Agents (Heuristic and LLM-powered) for vulnerability discovery and pattern matching, Investigator Agent for deep analysis with confidence scoring and CWE classification, PoV Generator Agent for exploit script creation with language-specific generation, and Validation/Docker Agents for multi-stage verification including static analysis, unit testing, and sandbox execution. Include agent interfaces, shared data structures, tool integration patterns, and state management. Provide implementation details for agent-specific algorithms, external tool integration (CodeQL, Semgrep, Docker), and performance optimization techniques. Address agent customization, extension points, and best practices for developing new agent types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Learning Store & Database
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed learning store and database documentation for AutoPoV focused on SQLite-based data persistence and model performance tracking. Document the learning.db schema with two main tables: investigations (vulnerability analysis records) and pov_runs (Proof-of-Vulnerability execution outcomes). Explain data models including investigation records with fields for scan_id, cwe, filepath, language, source, verdict, confidence, model, cost_usd, and timestamp. Detail PoV execution records with scan_id, cwe, model, cost_usd, success flag, validation_method, and timestamp. Document data recording operations, query patterns, and aggregation functions for performance analytics. Include model recommendation algorithms based on historical performance metrics, cost optimization strategies, and confirmation rates. Explain data summarization functions, statistical analysis queries, and reporting capabilities. Address data lifecycle management, retention policies, and cleanup procedures. Document prompt cache functionality and its role in reducing API costs. Provide guidance for database maintenance, backup strategies, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AutoPoV's core UI components focused on the specialized components that handle vulnerability scanning workflows and results display. Document the AgentPipeline component for visualizing multi-agent workflows and progress tracking. Explain the ResultsDashboard component for displaying vulnerability findings with filtering, sorting, and severity-based presentation. Detail the ScanForm component for code input methods including Git repository URLs, ZIP file uploads, direct code pasting, and directory scanning options. Cover the WebhookSetup component for GitHub/GitLab integration configuration. Document utility components like SeverityFilter, SeverityBadge, LiveLog, and ModelSelector with their props, events, and usage patterns. Include component composition patterns, prop interfaces, event handling, and integration with backend APIs. Provide code examples showing component usage, customization options, and styling approaches using Tailwind CSS classes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed deployment and orchestration documentation for AutoPoV containerized environment. Document deployment strategies for different environments (development, staging, production) with configuration variations. Explain environment-specific docker-compose overrides, resource allocation tuning, and security hardening measures. Detail the startup sequence, service dependencies, and health monitoring approaches. Include deployment automation scripts, container cleanup procedures, and maintenance workflows. Document scaling considerations, load balancing strategies, and high availability configurations. Address backup and recovery procedures for containerized deployments, including volume snapshotting and service restoration. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues and container runtime problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Maintenance Procedures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed maintenance procedures documentation for AutoPoV focused on system upkeep and operational housekeeping. Document Docker container cleanup procedures including stopped container removal, unused images, and dangling volumes. Explain disk space monitoring and analysis tools for identifying large data accumulations in ChromaDB, scan results, and logs. Detail ChromaDB maintenance including database optimization, index rebuilding, and storage cleanup. Include Ollama model management procedures for model provisioning, updates, and cleanup of unused models. Document system monitoring procedures including scan progress tracking, resource utilization monitoring, and performance metrics collection. Provide maintenance schedules for regular cleanup tasks, log rotation, and system health checks. Address troubleshooting common maintenance issues including stuck scans, container conflicts, and resource exhaustion scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Agent Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for extending AutoPoV's agent ecosystem with custom implementations. Document the agent interface contract, required methods, and state management patterns. Explain the agent registration system, lifecycle management, and integration with the LangGraph orchestration framework. Detail best practices for agent development including error handling, logging, resource management, and performance optimization. Include step-by-step tutorials for creating specialized agents such as domain-specific vulnerability detectors, custom validation agents, or language-specific analyzers. Document advanced patterns like agent collaboration, conditional routing, and dynamic agent instantiation. Address testing strategies, debugging techniques, and integration with the existing agent ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the agent development workflow in AutoPoV. Explain the step-by-step process of creating custom agents including project setup, interface implementation, testing strategies, and integration procedures. Document best practices for agent development such as error handling patterns, logging conventions, resource management, and performance optimization. Include practical examples from existing agents like the Ingestion Agent and Scout Agents. Provide templates and boilerplate code for common agent types. Address debugging techniques, unit testing approaches, and integration testing with the broader agent ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cost Control Mechanisms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's cost control and optimization systems. Document the MAX_COST_USD budget management, real-time cost tracking mechanisms, and token consumption monitoring per model. Explain the cost calculation methodology for different LLM providers, including token pricing, context window costs, and embedding operations. Detail the cost-aware routing strategies that automatically select more cost-effective models for simpler tasks. Include practical examples of cost configuration, budget allocation across different scan phases, and cost optimization techniques. Address advanced topics like cost prediction algorithms, dynamic budget adjustment based on scan progress, and integration with external cost monitoring systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Discovery & Scout Agents
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AutoPoV's vulnerability discovery and scout agents including Heuristic Scout for pattern-based detection, LLM Scout for AI-powered candidate generation, and Agentic Discovery for hybrid approach combining multiple strategies. Document the heuristic pattern matching algorithms, regular expression-based vulnerability detection, LLM prompting strategies for vulnerability candidate generation, confidence scoring mechanisms, and multi-agent coordination patterns. Include implementation details for pattern databases, vulnerability signature matching, LLM integration patterns, and hybrid discovery workflows. Provide examples of discovery pipelines, pattern configuration, and agent collaboration strategies for optimal vulnerability detection coverage.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for AutoPoV's system design focused on the high-level architecture, component relationships, and design patterns. Describe the LangGraph-based agent orchestration system, stateful workflow management, and conditional routing mechanisms. Document component interactions between FastAPI backend, specialized agents, external tools (CodeQL, Docker, ChromaDB), and the learning store. Explain technical decisions including the choice of LangGraph for state management, vector database integration for semantic search, and multi-stage validation pipeline. Include infrastructure requirements covering Docker containerization, LLM provider integration, and storage needs. Provide system context diagrams showing data flows from code ingestion through exploit validation. Address cross-cutting concerns like security (rate limiting, sandboxing), monitoring (real-time streaming), and scalability considerations. Document technology stack including FastAPI, LangGraph, LangChain, ChromaDB, and React frontend.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data flow documentation for AutoPoV's multi-stage vulnerability detection pipeline focused on information movement and transformation throughout the system. Trace the complete data journey from initial codebase ingestion through vector database embedding, vulnerability discovery, investigation, exploit generation, and validation. Document the state transitions in ScanState and VulnerabilityState objects, data serialization and deserialization patterns, and inter-component data sharing mechanisms. Explain the vector database integration for semantic search, the learning store for performance tracking, and token/cost tracking across the entire pipeline. Detail the parallel processing data flow, batch operations, and thread-safe data handling. Include practical examples of data transformation at each stage, error propagation patterns, and data validation mechanisms. Address performance considerations, caching strategies, and data lifecycle management from ingestion to final results delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's administrative endpoints focused on comprehensive coverage of scan management, cache operations, and system maintenance functions. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and response schemas for scan-control (/api/scan/{scan_id}/cancel, /api/scan/{scan_id}/stop, /api/scan/{scan_id}/delete), cleanup (/api/scans/cleanup), cache-management (/api/cache/stats, /api/cache/clear), and health-check (/api/health) endpoints. Include detailed parameter specifications, response formats, and administrative permissions requirements. Document scan lifecycle management including cancellation, forced stopping, and deletion operations with their respective use cases and limitations. Provide practical examples for each administrative operation showing proper authorization headers and expected responses. Include cache statistics retrieval, cache clearing procedures, and system health monitoring capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security configuration documentation for AutoPoV focused on access control, authentication, and security hardening. Document the two-tier authentication system including API key management, rate limiting configuration, and webhook security settings. Explain security headers, CORS configuration, and allowed origins setup. Document Docker sandbox security configuration, container isolation settings, and resource limits. Include security best practices for production deployments, API key rotation strategies, and webhook signature verification. Address security monitoring, audit logging, and common security vulnerabilities mitigation. Provide configuration examples for different security levels and threat models.

wiki_catalogs.name: Learning & Routing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's learning and routing system focused on the intelligent model selection and performance optimization mechanisms. Document the Policy Agent functionality for automatic model routing based on task complexity, historical performance data, and cost-benefit analysis. Provide examples of learning store schema, query patterns for performance analysis, and integration with the agent graph orchestration. Document the self-improving loop where agent decisions are stored and used to optimize future scans.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Navigation & Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's page-level components and navigation system focused on the different views and user workflows. Document the Home page as the main entry point with scan initiation forms and quick access controls. Explain the Results page for detailed vulnerability analysis, finding review, and export functionality. Detail the Settings page for configuration management including API keys, model selection, and security settings. Cover the History page for scan tracking and previous analysis review. Document the ScanManager page for bulk operations and scan scheduling. Explain the ScanProgress page with real-time monitoring and live log streaming. Include the Docs and Policy pages for documentation and terms of service. Address page-specific state management, data loading patterns, route parameters handling, and navigation flow between pages. Provide examples of programmatic navigation, route guards, and page transition effects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sandbox Execution & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed sandbox execution and security documentation for AutoPoV's exploit testing framework. Document the Docker runner agent implementation for safe exploit execution, including container isolation, resource limitations (512MB RAM, 1 CPU, 60s timeout), and security restrictions. Explain the exploit container lifecycle management, including creation, execution, and cleanup procedures. Detail volume mounting strategies for code injection, temporary file handling, and result extraction. Document security hardening measures, network isolation techniques, and privilege restrictions applied to exploit containers. Include container monitoring, resource tracking, and automatic termination mechanisms. Address common security considerations, vulnerability mitigation strategies, and best practices for safe exploit testing. Provide troubleshooting guidance for sandbox execution failures and security policy violations.

Include performance optimization techniques for LLM inference, vector database queries, and parallel processing of multiple scans. Document capacity planning guidelines including expected resource consumption per scan type, peak usage patterns, and growth projections. Address cost optimization strategies including model selection for different workloads, resource auto-scaling policies, and efficient Docker image management. Provide monitoring and alerting setup for scaling triggers and performance degradation detection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility & Configuration Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's utility and configuration endpoints focused on comprehensive coverage of system configuration, model management, and operational utilities. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and response schemas for configuration retrieval (/api/config), health monitoring (/api/health), and replay operations (/api/scan/{scan_id}/replay). Include detailed response formats for system configuration including model availability, feature flags, and operational status. Document model routing configuration, discovery modes, and performance settings. Provide practical examples for configuration queries, health checks, and scan replay operations. Include system capability detection, model validation, and operational readiness verification. Address configuration best practices, model selection strategies, and system optimization recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation & Verification Agents
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's multi-stage validation agents including Static Validator for code analysis, Unit Test Runner for automated testing, Docker Runner for sandbox execution, and Verifier for quality assurance. Document the static analysis integration with CodeQL and Semgrep, unit test harness execution, Docker containerization for safe exploit testing, and comprehensive validation workflows. Include detailed coverage of validation pipelines, result interpretation, quality metrics, error handling, and integration with the broader agent orchestration system. Provide implementation examples showing validation sequences, tool integration patterns, and quality assurance processes for ensuring reliable vulnerability findings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration documentation for AutoPoV focused on environment-driven settings management and customization options. Document all environment variables including core settings (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, ADMIN_API_KEY, MODEL_MODE, ROUTING_MODE), agent routing configurations, scout agent parameters, and CodeQL integration settings. Explain the three routing modes (auto, fixed, learning) with detailed behavioral descriptions and use case recommendations. Include security settings covering rate limiting, two-tier authentication system, and Docker sandbox configuration. Document cost control mechanisms including MAX_COST_USD caps and per-agent cost limits. Provide configuration examples for different deployment scenarios including online/offline LLM modes, development vs production setups, and performance tuning options. Address configuration validation, environment variable precedence, and troubleshooting common configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AutoPoV's React-based frontend application focused on the web interface architecture and user experience. Describe the component structure including the main App shell, navigation system, and specialized components like AgentPipeline for workflow visualization and ResultsDashboard for vulnerability display. Document the state management approach, routing configuration, and real-time progress streaming integration. Include detailed component documentation covering ScanForm for code input, Settings page for configuration management, and Results page for vulnerability review. Provide guidelines for component customization, styling with Tailwind CSS, and responsive design considerations. Document the integration with backend APIs, WebSocket connections for live updates, and user authentication flow. Address accessibility compliance, cross-browser compatibility, and performance optimization techniques for the React application.

wiki_catalogs.name: CLI Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CLI documentation for AutoPoV's command-line interface focused on automation and integration capabilities. Document all CLI commands including scan operations for Git repositories, local directories, and code pasting with model selection and CWE filtering options. Include results viewing commands for displaying agent findings, report generation for PDF/JSON formats, and history management for scan tracking. Document key management commands for API key generation and listing. Provide detailed usage examples for common automation scenarios including batch processing, CI/CD integration, and scripted vulnerability assessment workflows. Include command-line argument specifications, output formats, and error handling. Address integration patterns with other tools, scripting best practices, and performance considerations for large-scale usage. Document environment variable usage and configuration options available through CLI.

wiki_catalogs.name: Docker & Containerization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive Docker and containerization documentation for AutoPoV focused on deployment architecture and sandbox execution. Document the multi-container Docker Compose setup including backend API service, frontend dashboard, and supporting services. Explain container configuration including resource limits (512MB RAM, 1 CPU, 60s timeout), network isolation, and security restrictions for exploit execution. Detail the Docker runner agent implementation for safe exploit testing, including container lifecycle management, volume mounting, and cleanup procedures. Include deployment strategies for different environments (development, staging, production) with configuration variations. Document container orchestration, health checks, logging configuration, and monitoring approaches. Address troubleshooting container-related issues, resource optimization, and scaling considerations. Provide guidance for customizing Docker images, extending container functionality, and integrating with existing container platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data management documentation for AutoPoV focused on vector database integration, learning store functionality, and data persistence. Document ChromaDB configuration for semantic search and retrieval, including embedding generation, similarity search, and vector store management. Explain the SQLite-based learning store (learning.db) schema covering investigation records, PoV execution outcomes, and performance tracking metrics. Detail data flow from code ingestion through vector embedding to agent RAG context retrieval. Include data validation rules, indexing strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Document data lifecycle management including retention policies, cleanup procedures, and backup strategies. Address data security considerations, access control for sensitive vulnerability information, and privacy compliance. Provide guidance for data analysis queries, performance monitoring, and troubleshooting data-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Authentication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and authentication documentation for AutoPoV focused on access control, rate limiting, and secure operations. Document the two-tier authentication system including Admin Key for management operations with HMAC-safe comparison and API Keys for agent operations with SHA-256 hashing. Explain rate limiting implementation with 10 agent runs per API key per 60 seconds and enforcement mechanisms. Detail webhook security including GitHub/GitLab HMAC signature verification, payload validation, and secure webhook endpoint configuration. Document Docker sandbox security including network isolation, resource limits, and container hardening. Include security best practices for API key management, environment variable protection, and secure deployment configurations. Address threat modeling, security audit considerations, and incident response procedures. Provide guidance for security monitoring, vulnerability assessment, and compliance requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing and quality assurance documentation for AutoPoV focused on test suite organization and quality assurance processes. Document the pytest-based test suite covering API endpoints, agent functionality, authentication mechanisms, and integration components. Explain unit testing strategy for individual components, integration testing for agent workflows, and end-to-end testing for complete scan pipelines. Include test execution procedures, continuous integration setup, and automated testing workflows. Document testing best practices including test data management, mock object usage for external dependencies, and performance testing approaches. Address code quality metrics, static analysis integration, and security testing procedures. Provide guidance for writing new tests, debugging test failures, and maintaining test coverage. Include testing environment setup, test database management, and troubleshooting common testing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Observability
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive monitoring and observability documentation for AutoPoV focused on system health, performance tracking, and operational visibility. Document health check endpoints, system metrics collection, and real-time progress streaming via Server-Sent Events. Explain monitoring implementation including scan progress tracking, agent state monitoring, and resource utilization metrics. Detail logging configuration, log aggregation strategies, and debugging approaches using LangSmith tracing. Include performance monitoring for LLM usage, Docker container metrics, and database performance indicators. Document alerting mechanisms, threshold configuration, and incident response procedures. Address operational dashboards, metrics visualization, and reporting capabilities. Provide guidance for troubleshooting system issues, performance optimization, and capacity planning. Include monitoring best practices for production deployments and scaling considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for AutoPoV focused on production deployment, scaling considerations, and maintenance procedures. Document production deployment strategies including Docker Compose configuration, environment-specific settings, and infrastructure requirements. Explain scaling approaches for high-volume usage including load balancing, horizontal scaling, and resource optimization. Detail maintenance procedures including system cleanup, Docker container management, disk space monitoring, and model provisioning. Include backup and recovery procedures for critical data including learning store, scan results, and configuration files. Document upgrade procedures, version management, and rollback strategies. Address operational tasks including log rotation, performance tuning, and capacity planning. Provide guidance for cloud deployment, container orchestration, and monitoring setup. Include troubleshooting common deployment issues and operational challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive advanced topics documentation for AutoPoV focused on expert-level usage, customization, and research applications. Document intelligent model routing and optimization including the Policy Agent's decision-making process, cost control strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Explain custom agent development including agent interface implementation, integration guidelines, and best practices for extending the agent ecosystem. Detail benchmarking capabilities including scan replay functionality, performance analysis tools, and comparative evaluation methods. Include research-oriented features such as agent learning analysis, performance tracking, and experimental configuration options. Document advanced configuration scenarios, troubleshooting complex issues, and system optimization techniques. Address integration patterns with external tools, API extension possibilities, and contribution guidelines for the open-source community. Provide guidance for academic research, red-teaming operations, and enterprise deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for AutoPoV focused on common issues, error resolution, and performance optimization. Document frequent problems including Docker container issues, LLM provider connectivity problems, API key authentication failures, and scan execution errors. Provide systematic debugging approaches for agent workflow issues, vector database problems, and webhook integration failures. Include performance tuning guidance for slow scans, memory usage optimization, and cost control strategies. Document error message interpretation, log analysis techniques, and diagnostic procedures. Address platform-specific issues for different operating systems and deployment environments. Include preventive measures, best practices for avoiding common pitfalls, and escalation procedures for complex issues. Provide community resources, support channels, and contribution opportunities for users needing additional assistance.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# AutoPoV: Autonomous Proof-of-Vulnerability Framework

## Project Introduction

AutoPoV is a sophisticated autonomous vulnerability research platform that implements a fully agentic security workflow using LangGraph. The system automates the entire vulnerability detection and exploitation process without human intervention, making it ideal for academic benchmarking, red-team research, and automated security pipeline integration.

### Purpose Statement
AutoPoV transforms traditional vulnerability assessment from a manual, tool-centric process into an intelligent, autonomous agent system that can discover, analyze, exploit, and validate security vulnerabilities across multiple programming languages and frameworks.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Autonomous Discovery**: Multi-agent system that independently identifies potential vulnerabilities
- **Proof-of-Vulnerability Generation**: Creates working exploit scripts for confirmed findings
- **Multi-Stage Validation**: Escalates from static analysis to unit testing to sandboxed execution
- **Self-Improving Agents**: Learns from past experiences to optimize future performance
- **Benchmarking Platform**: Enables systematic comparison of different LLM models and approaches

### Target Audience
- Security researchers and red teams
- Academic institutions studying autonomous security workflows
- Organizations integrating automated vulnerability assessment into CI/CD pipelines
- Penetration testers seeking comprehensive vulnerability discovery

## Technical Architecture

AutoPoV employs a sophisticated multi-agent architecture built on LangGraph, creating a stateful workflow where each stage operates as an independent agent with autonomous decision-making capabilities.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[User Input] --> B[FastAPI Backend]
    B --> C[Agent Graph Orchestrator]
    C --> D[Ingestion Agent]
    C --> E[Scout Agents]
    C --> F[Investigator Agent]
    C --> G[PoV Generator Agent]
    C --> H[Validation Agent]
    C --> I[Docker Runner Agent]
    
    D --> J[Vector Store]
    E --> J
    F --> J
    
    H --> K[Static Analysis]
    H --> L[Unit Tests]
    H --> M[Docker Execution]
    
    I --> N[Sandboxed Environment]
    
    style C fill:#e1f5fe
    style J fill:#f3e5f5
    style N fill:#fff3e0
```

### Component Breakdown

**Backend Layer**: Built with FastAPI providing REST API endpoints, authentication, and WebSocket streaming for real-time updates.

**Agent Graph**: Central orchestrator using LangGraph to manage state transitions between different vulnerability detection stages.

**Agent Pool**: Specialized agents for different aspects of vulnerability research:
- Ingestion Agent: Codebase analysis and embedding
- Scout Agents: Pattern-based and LLM-driven discovery
- Investigator Agent: Deep analysis with confidence scoring
- PoV Generator Agent: Exploit script creation
- Validation Agent: Multi-stage verification
- Docker Runner Agent: Safe exploit execution

**Storage Layer**: Persistent storage for scan results, learning data, and configuration.

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key design patterns:

- **State Machine Pattern**: LangGraph-based workflow management
- **Strategy Pattern**: Multiple validation approaches (static в†’ unit в†’ Docker)
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic agent instantiation based on configuration
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time progress streaming via Server-Sent Events
- **Repository Pattern**: Unified data access abstraction

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**FastAPI Application** (`app/main.py`)
- REST API surface with comprehensive vulnerability scanning endpoints
- Real-time progress streaming via Server-Sent Events
- Webhook integration for automated triggering
- Comprehensive authentication and rate limiting

**Configuration Management** (`app/config.py`)
- Environment-driven settings with Pydantic validation
- Support for both online (OpenRouter) and offline (Ollama) LLM modes
- Flexible model routing and cost control mechanisms

### Core Modules

**Agent Graph Orchestration** (`app/agent_graph.py`)
- Stateful workflow management using LangGraph
- Conditional routing based on finding confidence and validation results
- Parallel processing capabilities for efficient multi-finding analysis
- Comprehensive logging and progress tracking

**Agent Implementations** (`agents/`)
- Modular agent architecture with clear separation of concerns
- Extensible design supporting custom agent development
- Integrated tool orchestration (CodeQL, Docker, ChromaDB)

### Configuration Approach

**Environment-Based Configuration** (`app/config.py`)
- Comprehensive settings management with validation
- Support for multiple LLM providers and models
- Runtime configuration validation and error handling
- Docker-aware configuration for containerized deployments

**Frontend Configuration** (`frontend/package.json`)
- React-based web interface with modern UI/UX
- Real-time dashboard with progress visualization
- Comprehensive reporting and result presentation

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework**:
- FastAPI for REST API development
- LangGraph for stateful agent orchestration
- Pydantic for configuration validation

**LLM Integration**:
- OpenRouter for online model access
- Ollama for local model execution
- LangChain ecosystem for LLM interactions

**Data Management**:
- ChromaDB for vector storage and retrieval
- SQLite for learning store persistence
- JSON serialization for scan results

**Development Tools**:
- Docker for containerized execution
- Playwright for automated testing
- Vite for frontend development

### Integration Points

**External Tool Integration**:
- CodeQL for static analysis
- Docker for exploit execution
- Git for repository access
- ChromaDB for semantic search

**API Integration**:
- OpenRouter API for LLM access
- GitHub/GitLab webhooks for automation
- Real-time streaming via WebSocket connections

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant API
    participant AgentGraph
    participant Agents
    participant Tools
    
    User->>+API: Scan Request
    API->>+AgentGraph: Create Scan
    AgentGraph->>+Agents: Ingest Codebase
    Agents->>+Tools: CodeQL Analysis
    Tools-->>-Agents: Analysis Results
    Agents->>+Agents: Investigate Findings
    Agents->>+Tools: Generate PoV
    Tools-->>-Agents: Exploit Script
    Agents->>+Tools: Validate in Docker
    Tools-->>-Agents: Validation Result
    Agents-->>-AgentGraph: Final Results
    AgentGraph-->>-API: Scan Complete
    API-->>-User: Results
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Source Code Ingestion**
- Git repository cloning with branch/commit support
- ZIP file upload and extraction
- Direct directory scanning
- Raw code paste functionality

**Advanced Vulnerability Detection**
- 20+ CWE category coverage across multiple languages
- Hybrid discovery combining static analysis and LLM reasoning
- Confidence scoring and prioritization
- Real-time streaming of agent progress

**Proof-of-Vulnerability Generation**
- Automated exploit script creation
- Multi-stage validation (static в†’ unit в†’ Docker)
- Sandbox execution with safety controls
- Comprehensive result documentation

**Intelligent Model Routing**
- Automatic model selection based on task complexity
- Performance optimization through learning store
- Cost control and budget management
- Support for both online and offline LLM modes

### Implementation Highlights

**Agent Workflow Orchestration** (`app/agent_graph.py`)
- Stateful workflow management with conditional routing
- Parallel processing for efficiency
- Comprehensive error handling and recovery
- Real-time progress tracking and logging

**Scout Agent System** (`agents/heuristic_scout.py`, `agents/llm_scout.py`)
- Pattern-based vulnerability discovery
- LLM-driven candidate generation
- Multi-strategy approach for comprehensive coverage
- Cost-benefit analysis for efficient processing

**Docker Execution Framework** (`agents/docker_runner.py`)
- Isolated sandbox execution
- Language-specific runtime resolution
- Comprehensive logging and monitoring
- Safety controls and resource limits

**CLI Interface** (`cli/autopov.py`)
- Command-line vulnerability scanning
- Batch processing capabilities
- Integration with existing security workflows
- Comprehensive reporting options

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> CodeIngestion
    CodeIngestion --> Discovery
    Discovery --> Investigation
    Investigation --> ConfidenceCheck
    ConfidenceCheck --> GeneratePoV
    ConfidenceCheck --> SkipFinding
    GeneratePoV --> Validation
    Validation --> PoVExecution
    PoVExecution --> Confirmed
    PoVExecution --> Failed
    Failed --> Refinement
    Refinement --> Validation
    Confirmed --> NextFinding
    SkipFinding --> NextFinding
    NextFinding --> Investigation
    NextFinding --> [*]
```

**Security Architecture**
- Two-tier authentication system (Admin/API keys)
- Rate limiting and request throttling
- Docker sandboxing for exploit execution
- Result cleanup and retention policies

**Benchmarking Capabilities**
- Scan replay functionality for model comparison
- Performance metrics collection
- Cost tracking and optimization
- Historical analysis and reporting

**Research-Focused Features**
- LangSmith tracing integration
- Comprehensive logging and audit trails
- Extensible agent architecture
- Academic benchmarking support

---

**Sources:**
- [app/main.py](app/main.py)
- [app/config.py](app/config.py)
- [app/agent_graph.py](app/agent_graph.py)
- [agents/__init__.py](agents/__init__.py)
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt)
- [frontend/package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [agents/investigator.py](agents/investigator.py)
- [agents/docker_runner.py](agents/docker_runner.py)
- [cli/autopov.py](cli/autopov.py)

</blog>
